cases.prompt: harden our next.config for production
set up linting for our next.js app
the next build bundle is huge, analyze it
fix the RSC / use client boundaries in this app
enable react strict mode and typed routes
next.js app quality checkup
images load slowly in our nextjs app, fix the setup
app router best practices for this project
make our next app production-ready
set up biome for a node library
configure vitest coverage
package this library for npm
add a node version matrix to CI
deploy the app to vercel
write a new react component for the dashboard
audit npm dependencies
cut a release of our design system
make this next.js app production-ready
expected_behavior: audits the current next.config and applies hardening with rationale (strict mode, typed routes where stable, image config, output mode fit for the deploy target)
uses eslint-config-next flat config for Next/RSC rules instead of generic lint setups
runs bundle analysis and reports the top actionable wins rather than generic advice
verifies next build passes after every change

cases.prompt: we get hydration and 'use client' errors everywhere
expected_behavior: explains the server/client boundary model and finds the actual offending imports rather than sprinkling use client
moves client-only code behind minimal client components; keeps data fetching server-side
adds the lint rules that catch boundary violations going forward

cases.prompt: nextjs bundle is 2MB of JS, shrink it
expected_behavior: measures first (bundle analyzer) and identifies the heaviest imports with evidence
applies real fixes: dynamic imports, server components for static parts, tree-shakeable imports, image/font optimization
